title: Codex Task

result.rawOutput:
**Summary**
This MR adds a `domain` alias (`feishu` / `lark`) for Feishu bot credentials, resolves it to the corresponding base URL, and threads that through `FeishuClientBuilder` for `ConnectionManager`-managed bots. It also adds API-time validation for `credentials.domain`, but the implementation is incomplete and has a few semantic problems.

**Correctness**
- `feishu_domain` is added to config/settings, but the legacy static-config path does not actually use it. `[settings.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/config/settings.py):33` says `feishu_domain` should drive `feishu_base_url`, yet `[settings.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/config/settings.py):165` and `[settings.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/config/settings.py):166` just copy `fs.domain` and `fs.base_url` independently, and the runtime still uses only `[app.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/api/app.py):177` and `[app.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/api/app.py):1651`. Result: `[feishu] domain = "lark"` without also setting `base_url` still talks to `open.feishu.cn`.
- `base_url` silently wins over `domain` in `[feishu_domain.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_domain.py):76`, `[feishu_domain.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_domain.py):89`, and `[feishu_domain.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_domain.py):100`. That means a bot/config can store `domain="lark"` and still use Feishu if a stale `base_url` is present. `_validate_feishu_credentials()` in `[bot_instance_api.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/api/bot_instance_api.py):28` does not check for this contradiction.
- Updating a Feishu bot just to add `credentials.domain` can break the bot because the update path replaces the whole credential bag. `[bot_instance_api.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/api/bot_instance_api.py):264` does `credentials = dict(body.credentials)`, and restart is triggered at `[bot_instance_api.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/api/bot_instance_api.py):271`. If the request only sends `{"domain": "lark"}`, `app_id`/`app_secret` are dropped and startup fails in `[client_builders.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/core/client_builders.py):39` and `[connection_manager.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/core/connection_manager.py):191`.

**Edge Cases**
- `normalize_domain()` is not actually safe for arbitrary input. It calls `value.strip()` in `[feishu_domain.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_domain.py):46`, so a truthy non-string like `123` will raise instead of falling back. The API rejects non-string `domain`, but malformed DB/TOML data would still blow up.
- Unknown `domain` values silently fall back to Feishu in `[feishu_domain.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_domain.py):41` and `[feishu_domain.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_domain.py):49`. That is inconsistent with the CRUD API, which rejects bad aliases, and it means non-API config paths fail open to the China endpoint.

**Security**
- The new `domain` validation is bypassable because only `domain` is validated. `[bot_instance_api.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/api/bot_instance_api.py):37` only inspects `credentials["domain"]`, while `[feishu_domain.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_domain.py):93` returns any truthy `base_url` unchanged. That value is then used by `[client_builders.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/core/client_builders.py):58` and `[feishu_client.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_client.py):117` for auth/API requests. If bot creators are not fully trusted, this remains an SSRF surface.
- The WS path has the same trust issue: `[client_builders.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/core/client_builders.py):86` passes the resolved `base_url` into `[feishu_ws.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_ws.py):76`, which only strips `/open-apis`.

**Backward Compatibility**
- Existing bot-instance records with no `domain` field should continue to work in the `ConnectionManager` path. `[feishu_domain.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_domain.py):70` and `[feishu_domain.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu_domain.py):102` default to Feishu, and `[bot_instance_api.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/api/bot_instance_api.py):38` allows omission.
- The gap is legacy config: adding the new `domain` field in TOML/env does not actually enable Lark unless `base_url` is also changed manually.

**Suggestions**
- Centralize all Feishu/Lark resolution through one strict helper and use it in both `ConnectionManager` and legacy bootstrap. The minimum fix is to derive `feishu_base_url` from `feishu_domain` in `[settings.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/config/settings.py):145` when `base_url` is not explicitly overridden.
- Validate `base_url` together with `domain` in `[bot_instance_api.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/api/bot_instance_api.py):28`. If both are present, either require them to agree or reject arbitrary hosts entirely.
- Make `normalize_domain()` type-safe and distinguish unset from invalid. `None`/empty can default; invalid or non-string values should raise or return an explicit error path.
- Add tests for the uncovered paths: legacy TOML/env config, conflicting `domain` + `base_url`, non-string `domain`, and update flows that change only `domain`. Current tests cover builder wiring and create-time validation, but not these cases.

**Verdict**
`request-changes`

Review is based on diff plus surrounding code inspection; I did not execute the branch’s test suite from this read-only checkout.
